title: connector CLI generates synthetic entrypoints per variant
description: For each discovered `*.preview.tsx` file and each of its `PreviewConfig.variants`, generate a synthetic Vite entrypoint file that imports the component, imports `preview-kit`'s `mount()`, and calls it with the specific variant id — one buildable HTML/JS entry per component variant.
acceptanceCriteria: For a `button.preview.tsx` with variants `['default', 'destructive']`, the CLI generates two synthetic entry modules (e.g. in a temp `.systembook/entries/` directory), one per variant

Each synthetic entry imports the real component from its original file location, imports `mount` from `@systembook/preview-kit`, and calls `mount(rootEl, config, Component, { variantId })` with the variant baked in
Each synthetic entry has a matching minimal HTML shell (a `<div id="root">` and a script tag referencing the entry) suitable as a Vite entrypoint
Generated entries are written to a git-ignorable temp directory, not committed into the target repo
Running generation twice in a row (idempotent) produces the same set of entries without leftover stale files from a previous component set

steps.description: Design the synthetic entry naming scheme
steps.details: Use a deterministic naming pattern like `.systembook/entries/{componentName}--{variantId}/index.tsx` and a sibling `index.html`.

steps.description: Generate the entry TSX file per variant
steps.details: Template out a file that does: `import Component from '<relative path to original file's component export>'; import config from '<relative path to *.preview.tsx>'; import { mount } from '@systembook/preview-kit'; mount(document.getElementById('root')!, config, Component, { variantId: '<variantId>' });`.

Template a minimal `index.html` with `
` and ``.
Before writing new entries, remove any pre-existing `.systembook/entries/` directory content so components/variants removed from source don't leave orphaned build entries.

steps.description: Test entry generation against the fixture repo
steps.details: Using the TASK-39 fixture, run generation and confirm the expected entry directories exist with correct TSX/HTML content for each variant of each valid `*.preview.tsx` file.

technicalNotes: This assumes each `*.preview.tsx` file's directory also exports (or sits next to) the actual React component being previewed — document this convention clearly since it affects how the generated entry resolves the component import path.
Keep generated entries out of the target repo's own git history via a `.gitignore` recommendation in the connector's setup docs (TASK-60/61).
